title: Adding captions to video and presentation content
what_it_is: Captions are text shown on screen that match spoken words in a video or presentation, appearing in time with the audio.
why_use_it: Captions let learners follow spoken content by reading rather than only listening — essential for anyone who is deaf or hard of hearing, and genuinely useful for anyone learning in a noisy space or in a second language.
how_to: Open your video in Stream (Microsoft's video platform, found inside Teams and SharePoint).
Select the three dots (⋯) above the video and choose Edit video details.
Go to the Captions tab and select Generate automatically.
Read through the generated caption text and fix any mistakes — automatic captions get names and technical terms wrong fairly often.
Select Publish once you're happy with it.
For live captions during a PowerPoint presentation, turn on Subtitle Settings from the Slide Show tab before you present.
